# Custom Theme System — Implementation Plan ## Context T3 Code currently has a minimal light/dark/system toggle (`useTheme.ts`) that applies a `.dark` class to `document.documentElement`. The goal is to extend this into a full named-theme system: three built-in theme presets ("t3-dark", "t3-light", "t3-midnight"), each with a complete color palette, applied to the app via CSS custom properties. The theme definition lives in `packages/shared` (browser-agnostic data only), the runtime hook lives in `apps/web`, and the selector surfaces in the existing Settings page. --- ## Architecture | Layer | Responsibility | |---|---| | `packages/shared/src/themes.ts` | Pure data: `Theme` type, 3 theme objects, `getTheme(id)`, `listThemes()` | | `apps/web/src/hooks/useColorTheme.ts` | Runtime: localStorage, CSS var application, dark-class management | | `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` | UI: color-swatch picker component | | `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | Wiring: replace Appearance radio buttons with `ThemeSelector` | | `apps/web/src/index.css` | CSS: add fallback values for new custom properties | The new `useColorTheme` hook **owns** the `.dark` class and all CSS custom property overrides. The existing `useTheme.ts` is left untouched for backward compatibility; the Settings UI will simply stop rendering the old radio buttons and render `ThemeSelector` instead. --- ## Step-by-Step Plan ### 1 — `packages/shared/src/themes.ts` (new file) Define the types and data. **No React, no DOM, no side-effects.** ```typescript export interface ThemeColors { background: string; foreground: string; accent: string; // maps to --primary / --ring sidebar: string; editor: string; terminal: string; statusBar: string; } export interface Theme { readonly id: string; readonly name: string; readonly isDark: boolean; readonly colors: ThemeColors; readonly fontFamily: string; readonly fontSize: string; } ``` Three built-in themes (absolute color values — no CSS var references — so the objects are self-contained and usable outside the browser): | Theme id | isDark | Notable character | |---|---|---| | `t3-dark` | true | Existing dark palette (neutral-950 base) | | `t3-light` | false | Existing light palette (white base) | | `t3-midnight` | true | Deep-navy blue-tinted scheme | ```typescript // t3-dark background: "#0d0d0d" foreground: "#f0f0f0" accent: "oklch(0.588 0.217 264)" sidebar: "#111111" editor: "#0d0d0d" terminal: "#0d0d0d" statusBar: "#161616" // t3-light background: "#ffffff" foreground: "#1f1f1f" accent: "oklch(0.488 0.217 264)" sidebar: "#f5f5f5" editor: "#ffffff" terminal: "#f5f5f5" statusBar: "#eeeeee" // t3-midnight background: "#0a0e1a" foreground: "#c8d8e8" accent: "oklch(0.65 0.18 240)" sidebar: "#0d1220" editor: "#0a0e1a" terminal: "#0a0e1a" statusBar: "#0d1220" ``` Export: ```typescript export function listThemes(): readonly Theme[] { return BUILT_IN_THEMES; } export function getTheme(id: string): Theme | undefined { return BUILT_IN_THEMES.find((t) => t.id === id); } export const DEFAULT_THEME_ID = "t3-dark"; ``` ### 2 — `packages/shared/package.json` (modify) Add the `./themes` subpath export following the exact existing pattern: ```json "./themes": { "types": "./src/themes.ts", "import": "./src/themes.ts" } ``` ### 3 — `apps/web/src/hooks/useColorTheme.ts` (new file) Mirror the exact `useSyncExternalStore` pattern from `useTheme.ts`. **localStorage key:** `"t3code:color-theme"` (default: `"t3-dark"`) **`applyColorTheme(theme: Theme, suppressTransitions = false)`** does: 1. Optionally add `no-transitions` class (same pattern as existing `applyTheme`) 2. Toggle `.dark` class based on `theme.isDark` 3. Set CSS custom properties on `document.documentElement.style`: - `--background` ← `theme.colors.background` - `--foreground` ← `theme.colors.foreground` - `--card` ← derived (set to `theme.colors.sidebar` as a reasonable mapping) - `--card-foreground` ← `theme.colors.foreground` - `--primary` and `--ring` ← `theme.colors.accent` - `--sidebar-background` ← `theme.colors.sidebar` (new var) - `--editor-background` ← `theme.colors.editor` (new var) - `--terminal-background` ← `theme.colors.terminal` (new var) - `--status-bar-background` ← `theme.colors.statusBar` (new var) 4. Set `document.documentElement.style.fontSize` ← `theme.fontSize` 5. Remove `no-transitions` on next frame (if suppressing) **Module-level FOUC prevention** (same as existing hook): ```typescript // Runs immediately on module import, before React renders applyColorTheme(getStoredColorTheme()); ``` **Hook return type** (use `as const` tuple pattern): ```typescript export function useColorTheme() { const snapshot = useSyncExternalStore(subscribe, getSnapshot); const setColorTheme = useCallback((id: string) => { localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, id); applyColorTheme(getThemeOrDefault(id), true); emitChange(); }, []); return { theme: snapshot, setColorTheme, availableThemes: listThemes() } as const; } ``` Cross-tab sync and system-preference patterns from `useTheme.ts` are replicated in the subscribe function (listen to `storage` events). **Helper:** `getThemeOrDefault(id)` returns `getTheme(id) ?? getTheme(DEFAULT_THEME_ID)!`. ### 4 — `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` (new file) A pure presentational + interactive component. No props needed (reads from hook directly). Layout: horizontal row of theme cards. Each card shows: - Theme name (text) - Three color swatches side-by-side: `background`, `sidebar`, `accent` (small colored divs) - Selected state: `border-primary/60 bg-primary/8` (same style as existing radio buttons in settings) - "Selected" badge when active (same badge style as existing settings) ```tsx import { useColorTheme } from "~/hooks/useColorTheme"; export function ThemeSelector() { const { theme, setColorTheme, availableThemes } = useColorTheme(); return (
{availableThemes.map((t) => { const selected = theme.id === t.id; return ( ); })}
); } ``` ### 5 — `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` (modify) In the existing "Appearance" section (lines 202–243): - **Remove** the existing `THEME_OPTIONS` const, the `resolvedTheme` usage from `useTheme`, and the radio-button group that renders system/light/dark options. - **Add** import for `ThemeSelector` component and `useColorTheme` hook. - **Replace** the radio-button group with ``. - The section header ("Appearance") and description ("Choose how T3 Code handles light and dark mode") can be updated to "Color Theme" and "Choose a color scheme for the editor." - Keep the rest of the settings page unchanged. The existing `useTheme` import can be removed from this file if it's no longer used; check for `resolvedTheme` usage in the "Active theme:" line and remove or replace with the new theme name. ### 6 — `apps/web/src/index.css` (modify) Add fallback CSS custom properties inside `:root` (before the `@variant dark` block) so that components can reference the new variables even before a theme is applied: ```css /* Color theme custom properties (applied via useColorTheme hook) */ --sidebar-background: var(--card); --editor-background: var(--background); --terminal-background: var(--background); --status-bar-background: var(--card); ``` Also add the corresponding `@variant dark` fallbacks (same values, they'll be overridden by the hook's inline style anyway). ### 7 — Early theme application (FOUC prevention) To ensure `useColorTheme.ts`'s module-level `applyColorTheme()` runs before the first React paint, add a side-effect import at the top of `apps/web/src/main.tsx` (after the CSS import): ```typescript import "~/hooks/useColorTheme"; // Apply color theme before first render ``` This mirrors the existing technique in `useTheme.ts` (also eagerly loaded via module-level code) and ensures no flash of unstyled content when the app loads. --- ## Critical Files | File | Action | |---|---| | `packages/shared/src/themes.ts` | Create | | `packages/shared/package.json` | Add `./themes` export entry | | `apps/web/src/hooks/useColorTheme.ts` | Create (mirror of `useTheme.ts`) | | `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` | Create | | `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | Modify Appearance section | | `apps/web/src/index.css` | Add 4 new CSS custom property fallbacks | | `apps/web/src/main.tsx` | Add side-effect import for FOUC prevention | **Reference implementations to follow exactly:** - `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` — `useSyncExternalStore` pattern, FOUC prevention, localStorage, cross-tab sync - `packages/shared/src/model.ts` — module structure, function exports - `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` lines 202–243 — existing Appearance UI pattern to replace --- ## Verification 1. **Unit check:** `bun typecheck` in repo root — all TS must compile clean. 2. **Lint check:** `bun lint` must pass with no errors. 3. **Manual test — theme switching:** - Navigate to Settings → Appearance. - Select each theme; verify the app background/text changes immediately. - Reload the page; verify the selected theme persists. - Open a second tab; change theme; verify both tabs update (cross-tab storage sync). 4. **Manual test — no FOUC:** - With `t3-midnight` selected, do a hard reload. The page should load already in midnight colors without flashing white or default dark first. 5. **Manual test — swatches:** - Each theme card shows 3 correctly colored swatches and a "Selected" badge on the active theme. 6. **Package export:** Confirm `import { getTheme, listThemes } from "@t3tools/shared/themes"` resolves in the web app and server if needed.

## Custom Theme System — Implementation Plan

Here's the complete plan I've designed after thorough exploration of the codebase.

### Key findings from codebase exploration:
- **Existing `useTheme.ts`**: Uses `useSyncExternalStore` pattern to manage light/dark/system preference and the `.dark` CSS class — I'll mirror this pattern exactly
- **`packages/shared`**: ESM subpath exports pointing directly to `.ts` source files (no build step) — same pattern for `./themes`
- **CSS system**: Tailwind v4 with `@theme inline` block + CSS custom properties on `:root`, dark mode via `.dark` class
- **Settings page**: Already has an Appearance section with light/dark/system radio buttons — we'll replace these with `ThemeSelector`

### What will be created/modified (7 files):

1. **`packages/shared/src/themes.ts`** (new) — `Theme` interface + `ThemeColors` interface + 3 built-in themes (t3-dark, t3-light, t3-midnight) + `getTheme(id)` + `listThemes()`

2. **`packages/shared/package.json`** (modify) — Add `"./themes"` subpath export

Here's a summary of what I found and the plan I've designed:

## Plan: Custom Theme System

### Findings from codebase exploration

- **Existing `useTheme.ts`** already handles light/dark/system via `useSyncExternalStore` — the new hook follows this exact pattern
- **CSS system**: Tailwind v4 + CSS custom properties on `:root`, dark mode via `.dark` class on `documentElement` — the theme system overrides these via `element.style.setProperty()`
- **`packages/shared`**: ESM subpath exports pointing directly to `.ts` source files (no build step needed)
- **Settings page**: Already has an Appearance section with light/dark/system radio buttons — we replace this UI with `ThemeSelector`

### 7 files to create/modify

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `packages/shared/src/themes.ts` | **New** — `Theme` + `ThemeColors` types, 3 built-in themes, `getTheme()`, `listThemes()` |
| `packages/shared/package.json` | **Modify** — add `./themes` subpath export |
| `apps/web/src/hooks/useColorTheme.ts` | **New** — `useSyncExternalStore` hook, localStorage key `t3code:color-theme`, applies CSS vars + dark class, FOUC prevention on module load |
| `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` | **New** — row of theme cards with 3 color swatches each, matches existing settings radio-button style |
| `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | **Modify** — replace the light/dark/system radio group with `<ThemeSelector />` |
| `apps/web/src/index.css` | **Modify** — add 4 new CSS property fallbacks (`--sidebar-background`, etc.) |
| `apps/web/src/main.tsx` | **Modify** — side-effect import of `useColorTheme.ts` for early FOUC prevention |

### Design decisions
- **t3-dark/t3-light** use colors matching the existing palette; **t3-midnight** introduces a deep navy scheme
- The new hook owns the `.dark` class and CSS overrides; existing `useTheme.ts` is left untouched
- Theme values use absolute colors (no CSS `var()` references) so the `themes.ts` module stays pure and server-safe
- The hook also sets 4 new semantic CSS vars (`--sidebar-background`, `--editor-background`, `--terminal-background`, `--status-bar-background`) for future component use

All done. Here's a summary of every change made:

---

## Implementation complete — 7 files changed

### `packages/shared/src/themes.ts` *(new)*
Defines the `ThemeColors` and `Theme` interfaces, three built-in theme objects (`t3-dark`, `t3-light`, `t3-midnight`), and exports `getTheme(id)`, `listThemes()`, and `DEFAULT_THEME_ID`. Pure data — no React, no DOM, usable in any context.

### `packages/shared/package.json` *(modified)*
Added `"./themes"` subpath export pointing to `./src/themes.ts`, matching the existing pattern for all other subpath exports.

### `apps/web/src/hooks/useColorTheme.ts` *(new)*
A `useSyncExternalStore`-based hook that mirrors the architecture of the existing `useTheme.ts`. Stores the selected theme ID in `localStorage` (`t3code:color-theme`), listens for cross-tab `storage` events, and exposes `{ theme, setColorTheme, availableThemes }`. The `applyColorTheme()` function sets CSS custom properties directly on `document.documentElement.style` (overriding the stylesheet) and toggles `.dark` based on `theme.isDark`. A module-level call prevents FOUC.

### `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` *(new)*
A radio-group component that renders a card per theme. Each card shows three colored swatches (background / sidebar / accent) and a "Selected" badge, using the exact same Tailwind classes and badge style as the existing settings radio buttons.

### `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` *(modified)*
Removed the old `THEME_OPTIONS` constant, the `useTheme` import, and the system/light/dark radio-button group. The "Appearance" section header was renamed to **"Color Theme"** and its body replaced with `<ThemeSelector />`.

### `apps/web/src/index.css` *(modified)*
Added four new CSS custom property fallbacks inside `:root` (before the `@variant dark` block): `--sidebar-background`, `--editor-background`, `--terminal-background`, `--status-bar-background`, each defaulting to the appropriate existing variable.

### `apps/web/src/main.tsx` *(modified)*
Added a side-effect import of `./hooks/useColorTheme` immediately after the CSS import, so the module-level `applyColorTheme()` call runs before the first React render.
